touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sent to Benjamin Mitchell introducing AskElephant's CRM automation capabilities. No response received.
Second LinkedIn message sent highlighting PropTech-specific use cases for automation. No response received.
Third LinkedIn message sent sharing case study on sales automation ROI. No response received.
Cold call attempt to Benjamin Mitchell. No answer, left voicemail about streamlining SolarMint's sales operations.
Cold email sent detailing how AskElephant helps PropTech teams automate CRM workflows and reduce manual data entry. No response received.
Follow-up email sent with video walkthrough of automation features relevant to SolarMint's sales process. No response received.

8-minute qualification call with Benjamin Mitchell. He expressed interest in automating call logging and CRM updates for his sales team. Mentioned they're currently evaluating Gong but concerned about implementation complexity. Agreed to demo next week.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your interest in CRM automation right now?
questions_asked.answer: Our sales team spends too much time on manual CRM updates. We need something that just works without heavy IT involvement.

questions_asked.question: Are you looking at any other solutions?
questions_asked.answer: We've been talking to Gong, but their implementation seems complex and expensive for a team our size.

objections: Concerned about implementation time and complexity based on Gong experience
next_steps: Schedule product demo for next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Email sent confirming demo scheduled for Tuesday at 2pm PT. Attached one-pager on typical implementation timeline (2-3 weeks) and included PropTech customer reference. Benjamin responded confirming attendance.

questions_asked.question: Can you share pricing ballpark before the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Happy to discuss pricing on the call - it depends on seat count and automation tier, but for a team your size typically $8-12K annually.

touches.ai_summary: 41-minute product demo with Benjamin Mitchell. Walked through call recording, automated CRM sync, and deal intelligence features. Benjamin engaged positively with automation capabilities but asked several questions about integrations and pricing. Noted budget is tight and will need to compare against Gong proposal. Mentioned he'll need to get VP Engineering's buy-in for implementation.

questions_asked.question: Does this integrate with our current tech stack - Salesforce and Slack?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, native Salesforce integration and Slack notifications are included in all plans.

questions_asked.question: What's the total cost for 12 seats with unlimited automation?
questions_asked.answer: For 12 seats on our Growth plan with unlimited automation, annual price is $11,880. We can discuss options to fit your budget.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this alongside you?
questions_asked.answer: Just me for now - I own the sales tools decision. I'll loop in our VP Engineering closer to implementation.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Gong for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: We're typically 30-40% lower than Gong for SMB teams, with faster implementation and no professional services fees.

objections: Budget is limited - need to stay under $10K annually
Still comparing against Gong proposal which has more features
next_steps: Benjamin to review internally this week
Follow up Friday with pricing options under $10K

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with revised proposal: 12 seats at $9,900 ACV (16% discount) to meet budget constraint. Included Gong comparison document highlighting implementation speed and cost advantages. Benjamin responded saying this looks more workable and he'll review with his team, aiming to decide by end of month.

questions_asked.question: Can you hold this pricing if we need another week to finalize?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, this pricing is valid through end of April.

next_steps: Benjamin reviewing internally through end of month
Check in next Tuesday for update
